title: A Yellow Mural the first bigscale Nounish mural in Cali Colombia
description: A Yellow Mural is a large-scale Nounish-inspired public artwork in Cali, Colombia, created by four Colombian artists to celebrate generosity, solidarity, community, and the Yellow Collective spirit.
details: A Yellow Mural brings together Colombian artists Yina Obando, Ritosinsermones, Carlos Arce Castaño, and Nadiecito to create a large-scale Nounish-inspired mural in Cali, Colombia, rooted in the local saying “Haz el bien sin mirar a quién.” Built around themes of generosity, equality, empathy, and social connection, the mural will reinterpret Nounish philosophy through the distinct visual languages of the participating artists, using yellow tones, contrasting details, and iconic elements like noggles. The project expands an existing mural opportunity into a more ambitious public collaboration with high-quality materials, video documentation, and an IRL community activation featuring music, dialogue, and local Nounish participation. Located in a visible public space, the mural is designed to bring color, intention, and shared cultural memory to the city while strengthening the relationship between Yellow Collective, Nouns, and Colombia’s creative community.
